questions.q: What is the capital of Germany?
How many players does each team have on the field in soccer?
Which gas makes up the majority of Earth's atmosphere?
Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
What is the capital city of Australia?
Which is the smallest of the world's oceans?
How many continents are there on Earth?
Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?
What is 15 × 4?
Which planet's rings are most easily seen from Earth through a small telescope?
What color is a strawberry?
What do cows give us to drink?
How many sides does a triangle have?
What do you wear on your head?
What season comes after summer?
What is 4 + 4?
What is 3 × 2?
Where do birds make their homes?
What color is a pumpkin?
How many days are in a week?
What is the capital of Argentina?
Which of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World is still standing today?
What does DNA stand for?
In which U.S. state is the Grand Canyon located?
Who wrote 'Hamlet'?
In which museum is the Mona Lisa displayed?
Approximately how far is the Moon from Earth?
Which African country has the largest population?
Which element has the chemical symbol 'K'?
What is the pH of pure water at 25°C?
What is the Laplace transform of f(t) = t²?
Every problem solvable in polynomial time (class P) is also in NP. This statement is:
choices: False — P and NP are disjoint
Unknown — this is the open P vs NP question

questions.q: The gradient ∇f at (1, 2) for f(x, y) = x²y + y³ is:
The eigenvalues of the matrix [[1, 2], [0, 3]] are:
For large n, which statement about n! and 2ⁿ is correct?
choices: They grow at the same asymptotic rate
Growth depends on the specific value of n

questions.q: For f(z) = eᶻ/(z² + 1), the residue at z = i is:
By the Central Limit Theorem, for large n the sample mean X̄ of n i.i.d. samples (mean μ, variance σ²) is approximately:
The time complexity of the naive algorithm for multiplying two n×n matrices is:
For a Poisson-distributed random variable X with mean λ, E[X(X − 1)] equals:
The Shannon entropy of a fair coin flip, measured in bits, is:
I have exactly two coins that together total 15 cents, and one of the coins is not a dime. What are the two coins?
What is 1/2 of 2/3 of 3/4 of 4/5 of 5/6 of 6/7 of 7/8 of 8/9 of 9/10 of 10?
Divide 30 by one half, then add ten. What is the result?
A barrel of water weighs 60 pounds. What must you put into it to make it weigh 30 pounds?
choices: Nothing — you must remove weight, not add it

questions.q: A man must cross a river with a fox, a chicken, and grain. His boat holds only himself and one other. The fox eats the chicken; the chicken eats the grain if left alone together. What is the minimum number of one-way crossings to get everything across safely?
How many times does the digit 1 appear when you write out every integer from 1 to 1,000 inclusive?
A man walks 1 mile due south, 1 mile due east, and 1 mile due north — and ends up exactly where he started. Where did he begin?
A man leaves home, turns left three times running, and arrives home to find two people wearing masks waiting for him. Who are they?
choices: The catcher and the home-plate umpire

questions.q: What is the next number in the sequence: 1, 2, 6, 24, 120, ___?
A lily pad doubles in area every day and covers an entire lake in exactly 48 days. How many days did it take to cover exactly half the lake?
choices: Cannot be determined without knowing the lake's size
